title: Security Outcomes, Threat Models, and Evidence · Language Lens: Crypto Go Security Properties
summary: A medical records product says data is encrypted but cannot name the protected asset, attacker capability, required property, trust boundary, or evidence that would falsify the claim. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the security properties increment in cryptographic outcome and evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Define confidentiality, integrity, authenticity, freshness, forward secrecy, availability, privacy, and accountability as separate observable outcomes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Security Properties · Theory Crypto Go Security Properties
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 7410 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a cryptographic claim on asset, adversary, properties, trust boundaries, authorization, and bounded evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Security Properties contract for the supplied data.
